[BUG] (The English text was translated using AI.) Version The version currently tagged as latest on Docker Hub, released 11 days ago. Problem description I pulled and deployed this project in Docker on my NAS and configured it according to the documentation. It is usable now, but regardless of which model I configure, the response speed is much faster than the displayed thinking time. When checking the logs, I can see the following errors repeatedly appearing while waiting for the answer: [2026-04-28T13:39:09.796Z] [WARN] [Render] MarkdownV2 validation issues detected: Unbalanced bold markers () [2026-04-28T13:39:10.834Z] [ERROR] Render failed with 400 error: Bad Request: can't parse entities: Character '>' is reserved and must be escaped with the preceding '\'. Clearing sentMessageIds to allow fresh messages. [2026-04-28T13:39:10.834Z] [ERROR] send message failed: 400 Bad Request: can't parse entities: Character '>' is reserved and must be escaped with the preceding '\' Troubleshooting analysis I tried troubleshooting this issue by adding the following settings to the config file: - SHOW_THINKING_TEXT="false" - EXPANDABLE_THINKING="false" After doing this, the thinking chain was no longer shown, the output speed improved significantly, and the logs also stopped showing the [ERROR] messages above. Additional information Below is the analysis from GPT-5.5 for reference: Conclusion: what you are experiencing is not MCP, and not just the old > escaping issue either. It looks more like a regression or residual bug in the message rendering / chunking / editing layer. latest being updated does not mean that this path is guaranteed to be working. > The evidence I found is: > - The repository only has two public issues directly related to this class of problem: - #4: SHOW_THINKING_TEXT / expandable thinking request - #5: 400 Bad Request: can't parse entities under EXPANDABLE_THINKING - The maintainer did later add a fix commit (39afcb5), but: - that fix only covers a specific quote / > handling path - there were later comments in the issue thread still reporting failures - The current sending pipeline still appears to be: - MarkdownV2 → HTML → Plain Text → Telegraph fallback - plus sendLongMessage chunking + edit/resend - with this kind of implementation, if one chunk still contains an unescaped > / * / _ / code block boundary, it can fail repeatedly with 400 errors, which explains: - the model thinking finishes quickly - the actual delay comes from rendering / retries / chunked sending > ### Why latest can still be broken 1. latest is only an image tag, not a guarantee of “bug-free” On Docker Hub, latest simply means the most recently built image. It does not mean: - it necessarily includes the fix you expected - it has no later regression - it covers every output path > 2. The project defaults to the thinking/quote path The README clearly shows: - SHOW_THINKING_TEXT default: true - EXPANDABLE_THINKING default: true - QUOTE_EXPANDABLE is also present > That means even without MCP, and regardless of model, as long as this rendering path is active, Telegram MarkdownV2 parsing can still fail. > 3. The “slow” behavior looks like repeated failures in the sending layer, not slow model inference This matches your symptoms: - token generation by the model itself is not slow - but the final message sent to Telegram must: - be rendered - be chunked - be edited/sent - retry/clean up after failures - so the user experience is that the answer arrives late and the logs keep repeating errors > ### Most likely root cause now The most suspicious factors are: 1. SHOW_THINKING_TEXT=true 2. EXPANDABLE_THINKING=true 3. A remaining edge-case bug in the long-message chunking + MarkdownV2 rendering fallback path > In particular, the following characters or structures are prone to causing problems: - > - * - _ - [ ] - code block boundaries - mixed quote blocks and normal text > ### Recommended minimal verification Before changing MCP or switching models, try disabling the output formatting path first: > - SHOW_THINKING_TEXT=false - EXPANDABLE_THINKING=false > If you want to be more aggressive, also set: - QUOTE_EXPANDABLE=false > ### What to observe If disabling these options causes: - the errors to drop significantly or disappear - the response speed to return to normal > then the issue is very likely in the Telegram formatting path for thinking/quote output, not in the model or networking. > ### My current judgment I currently lean toward this being a bug/regression in ChatGPT-Telegram-Workers’ output rendering path, rather than something that has already been fully fixed in latest. > Because the public information suggests: - this area has been fixed before - but a fix does not mean every path is fully covered - and the current code still relies on a complex MarkdownV2 parsing + chunked sending pipeline, which is inherently fragile --- **版本号** DockerHub中目前标记为latest的版本。发布于11天前。 **描述问题** 我于Nas的dockers中拉取并部署了该项目，根据文档进行配置，目前可以使用。但是无论我配置什么模型，每次回答的速度远超实际显示的思考时间。查询日志，可以看到在等待回答期间，以下日志内容在不断重复报错： [2026-04-28T13:39:09.796Z] [WARN] [Render] MarkdownV2 validation issues detected: Unbalanced bold markers (**) [2026-04-28T13:39:10.834Z] [ERROR] Render failed with 400 error: Bad Request: can't parse entities: Character '>' is reserved and must be escaped with the preceding '\'. Clearing sentMessageIds to allow fresh messages. [2026-04-28T13:39:10.834Z] [ERROR] send message failed: 400 Bad Request: can't parse entities: Character '>' is reserved and must be escaped with the preceding '\' **排错分析** 我尝试进行排查故障，在配置文件中，加入了SHOW_THINKING_TEXT= "false",EXPANDABLE_THINKING= "false"，结果虽然没有显示思维链，但输出结果的速度明显提高了太多，日志中也少了[ERROR]部分的报错。 **其他信息** 以下内容为gpt-5.5的分析： 结论：你现在遇到的不是 MCP，也不只是旧的 > 转义问题，而更像是“消息渲染/分段/编辑”层的回归或残留 bug。 latest 标签“更新了”≠“这个路径就一定正常了”。 我查到的证据是： - 仓库里只有两条和这类问题直接相关的公开 issue： - #4：SHOW_THINKING_TEXT / 可折叠 thinking 的需求 - #5：EXPANDABLE_THINKING 下 400 Bad Request: can't parse entities: Character '>' is reserved... - maintainer 之后确实做了一个修复提交（39afcb5），但： - 这个修复只覆盖了一条特定的 quote/> 处理路径 - issue 评论里后来仍有人反馈“我这里还是会报” - 代码里当前的发送链路仍然是： - MarkdownV2 → HTML → Plain Text → Telegraph 的复杂 fallback - 再叠加 sendLongMessage 的分块 + 编辑/重发 - 这类实现一旦某个 chunk 里还有未处理好的 > / * / _ / 代码块边界，就会反复 400，导致你看到： - 模型思考很快结束 - 真正耗时的是后面的渲染/重试/分片发送 --- 为什么 latest 还能坏 1) latest 只是镜像标签，不是“已验证无 bug”的保证 Docker Hub 上的 latest 只是“最近一次构建出来的镜像”，它不等于： - 一定包含你以为的那次修复 - 一定没有后续回归 - 一定覆盖了所有输出路径 2) 这个项目默认就会走“thinking/quote”路线 README 里已经写得很明确： - SHOW_THINKING_TEXT 默认 true - EXPANDABLE_THINKING 默认 true - 还有 QUOTE_EXPANDABLE 也就是说，即使你不接 MCP、即使任何模型都一样，只要这个输出格式化路径在跑，就可能触发 Telegram MarkdownV2 解析失败。 3) 你的“慢”很像发送层在反复失败，不像模型慢 这和你的现象完全一致： - 模型 token 输出本身不慢 - 但最终发给 Telegram 的消息需要： - 先渲染 - 再切块 - 再编辑/发送 - 失败后重试/清理 - 所以体感上就是：答案出来得很晚，而且日志一直刷错 --- 现在最可能的根因 我认为优先怀疑这三项： 1. SHOW_THINKING_TEXT=true 2. EXPANDABLE_THINKING=true 3. 长答案分块 + MarkdownV2 渲染 fallback 这条链路里仍有边界 bug 尤其是只要输出里出现这些符号，就容易出问题： - > - * - _ - [ ] - 代码块边界 - 引用块和普通文本混杂 --- 我建议你先做的最小验证 先不要碰 MCP，不要换模型，直接改输出格式： 先关掉这两个 - SHOW_THINKING_TEXT=false - EXPANDABLE_THINKING=false 如果你想更激进一点，再把： - QUOTE_EXPANDABLE=false --- 你应该观察什么 如果关闭后： - 报错明显减少 / 消失 - 回复速度恢复正常 那就可以基本确认： 问题在“thinking/quote 的 Telegram 格式化路径”，不是模型，也不是联网。 --- 目前我对你的情况的判断 我现在更倾向于： 这是 ChatGPT-Telegram-Workers 的输出渲染路径 bug / 回归，而不是“latest 已经修好”的问题。 因为仓库公开信息显示： - 这个区域确实修过 - 但修复并不等于完全覆盖 - 而且当前代码仍是高复杂度的 MarkdownV2 解析 + 分块发送逻辑，天然容易再炸 ---

[BUG] User import bugs # Description

After authenticating, the user expects to see progress they previously made that was associated with their auth account, e.g. from a different device. This is achieved by pulling previously synced data associated with their auth account from the server (authored via the `user: import` action). In some cases, this does not happen as expected. This is partly due to the fact that the list of available profiles to restore (exposed to authors as the `_auth_profiles` dynamic data list) explicitly excludes any user data rows that match the current `app_user_id`. The `app_user_id` used to be the only ID before auth was implemented (and must remain as an ID for those users who choose not to authenticate, on those deployments where this is an option).

# Broken user journeys

The following user journeys are broken, for example:
```
User signs in on device A
↓
User makes progress and syncs
↓
User signs out and deletes their data
OR
User deletes app
↓
User (reinstalls app and) signs back in
```

In these cases, the user will (at least sometimes) have the same `app_user_id` as they had before. This means that when they sign back in and expect to retrieve the data associated with their auth account, none will be available because the only entry has the same `app_user_id`, and is therefore excluded as a candidate. So the user does not see data from their previous session.

See: https://github.com/IDEMSInternational/open-app-builder/blob/3427d1d1f0bca61ef841fe750db326db3b52f08d/src/app/shared/services/auth/auth.service.ts#L120-L134

## Removing the filter

If we remove this restriction, and include data rows with the same `app_user_id` as the current user when retrieving candidates for import, then we open ourselves up to other edge cases where data can be lost. For example:
```
User signs in on Device A
↓
User makes progress and syncs
↓
User signs in on Device B
```

In this case, the user expects to see the data associated with the progress they made on Device A. However, user data is synced as part of sign-in flow, so the import candidate with the most recently updated data is an empty row that was just synced. It may be that we can navigate this by managing the sync operation.
See: https://github.com/IDEMSInternational/open-app-builder/blob/3427d1d1f0bca61ef841fe750db326db3b52f08d/src/app/shared/services/auth/auth.service.ts#L46-L48

# Reflections on current state of the feature

The current system was designed with the intention to support users moving from one device to the other, and we recognised that it had limitations vs a more thorough system for keeping user data in sync. It's currently very difficult to think through all the possible edge cases and user journeys, and it feels like we ultimately need to build that more thorough and robust system. A starting point for this would likely be to decisively treat the `auth_user_id` as the primary key on the data when it is available. I.e. syncs should be made using the `auth_user_id` as the primary key as opposed to the `app_user_id`. Without a merge strategy, this could lead to cases of data loss of some form, but that would be preferable to the current system. We also need to be careful about our syncing strategy, and ensure that we don't unintentionally sync data may overwrite desired data, or else that will be pulled down and overwrite desired local data.

Here "sync" refers to effectively a "push", which used to be the only kind of data operation, but we might want to rethink our naming too if we're implementing a more general push/pull syncing strategy.

# Additional context
I originally opened #3471 to attempt to resolve some of the related issues. On reflection, this doesn't address the core issues. But it is likely still desirable as an improvement to authoring the flow of pulling user data when signing in (see the description on the PR for full details).

In going through the code as I've been looking at adding in support for Helix Floor units, I noticed that `PROFILE_HX_STOMP` in `crates/core/src/device/catalog.rs:7` sets `preset_count: 128`, but the HX Stomp has 126 user presets.

I would expect `PROFILE_HX_STOMP.preset_count: 126`. It may also be good to think about a test case for device specific preset situations.

### What happened?

While tracking location in terminate state the onmotionchange event is not fired when stop the trip .
- Opened the app start the tracking . 
- Motion changed event fire for the stationary location and also fetched the location updates .
- Started moving the motion change event fires again with is moving true and start getting location updates while travelling . 
- Reached the destination after 1 hour of travelling , the stop timeout notification shown up with 300 s which is default timeout but event after 10 min of time passed the motion update event is not fired in the app .
- Also there is no location turn off notification shown up . just gets the event as onlocation even with moving as true . 

Summary : We are syncing the data manually and storing too , so when the tracking start saving location data locally on device, when  motion event is fired we sync the remaining data at once . But the event never fires so our data remains in the local storage only .

Bug: ## Bug: AsyncAPI schema generation crashes with `AttributeError: __pydantic_fields__` when a subscriber uses `fastapi.Depends` with a non-pydantic annotation                      
                                                                                                                                                                                     
  ### Environment                                                                                                                                                                    
  - `faststream == 0.6.7` (with `[rabbit]` extra)                                                                                                                                    
  - `fastapi == 0.118.0`                                                                                                                                                             
  - `fast-depends == 3.0.8`
  - `pydantic` v2, Python 3.12                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                     
  ### Summary
  When a subscriber registered via `RabbitRouter` (FastStream's FastAPI integration) has a parameter declared as a FastAPI dependency whose annotation is an arbitrary non-pydantic  
  class, requesting the AsyncAPI docs page returns **500 Internal Server Error** with `AttributeError: __pydantic_fields__. Did you mean: '__pydantic_fields_set__'?`.               
   
  In a plain FastAPI HTTP endpoint the same annotation works, because `Depends`-annotated parameters are excluded from OpenAPI schema generation. AsyncAPI generation in FastStream  
  does not seem to do the same exclusion — it includes every handler parameter into a dynamically created pydantic model and later fails when introspecting its fields.              
   
### Minimal reproduction                                                                                                                                                           
                  
  ```python
  # app.py
  from functools import lru_cache                                                                                                                                                    
  from typing import Annotated
                                                                                                                                                                                     
  from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI
  from faststream.rabbit import RabbitQueue                                                                                                                                          
  from faststream.rabbit.fastapi import RabbitRouter
  from pydantic import BaseModel                                                                                                                                                     
                  
                                                                                                                                                                                     
  class ExternalClient:
      """An arbitrary third-party class, NOT a pydantic model."""                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                     
      def __init__(self, token: str) -> None:                                                                                                                                        
          self.token = token                                                                                                                                                         
                  

  @lru_cache(maxsize=1)
  def _build_client() -> ExternalClient:                                                                                                                                             
      return ExternalClient(token="xxx")
                                                                                                                                                                                     
                  
  async def get_client() -> ExternalClient:
      return _build_client()                                                                                                                                                         
   
                                                                                                                                                                                     
  class Request(BaseModel):
      issue: str


  class Response(BaseModel):
      ok: bool                                                                                                                                                                       
   
                                                                                                                                                                                     
  router = RabbitRouter(url="amqp://guest:guest@localhost/")
                                                                                                                                                                                     
   
  @router.subscriber(RabbitQueue("in", durable=True))                                                                                                                                
  @router.publisher(RabbitQueue("out", durable=True))                                                                                                                                
  async def handler(
      message: Request,                                                                                                                                                              
      client: Annotated[ExternalClient, Depends(get_client)],
  ) -> Response:                                                                                                                                                                     
      return Response(ok=True)
                                                                                                                                                                                     
   
  app = FastAPI()                                                                                                                                                                    
  app.include_router(router)
```

  Run uvicorn app:app and open http://localhost:8000/asyncapi.                                                                                                                       
   
### Expected behavior                                                                                                                                                                  
                  
  AsyncAPI page renders, and the client parameter (marked with Depends(...)) is excluded from the message schema — same as in FastAPI's OpenAPI.                                     
   
  ### Actual behavior                                                                                                                                                                    
                  
  500 Internal Server Error. Traceback ends at:

```python
  File ".../faststream/specification/asyncapi/message.py", line 87, in get_model_schema
      params = {k: v for k, v in get_model_fields(call).items() if k not in exclude}                                                                                                 
  File ".../faststream/_internal/_compat.py", line 95, in get_model_fields                                                                                                           
      return model.__pydantic_fields__                                                                                                                                               
  AttributeError: __pydantic_fields__. Did you mean: __pydantic_fields_set__?

```
                                                                                                                                                                                     
  ### Suggested fix   
                                                                                                                                                                                     
  In parse_handler_params, exclude parameters that are resolved as `FastAPI/fast_depends` dependencies (same way HTTP OpenAPI excludes them), or pass their names into the exclude= set
   of `get_model_schema`.

## 💡 补充说明

codex的解释：
代码里确实有一个 enableActorEnhancement 开关元素，也就是我说的“演员页增强”总开关；但我刚才说得太笼统了，而且这版代码里还有个坑。

我刚核了构建文件，运行演员页增强的真实条件是：

actorEnhancement.enabled !== false && 当前页面是 /actors/...
但是设置保存时，actorEnhancement.enabled 不是直接用 enableActorEnhancement.checked 保存的，而是用了类似：

enabled = 自动应用标签开启 || 默认标签有勾选
所以可能出现这种情况：你开了“扫描新作品按钮”，但 actorEnhancement.enabled 仍然是 false，于是演员页增强整个不初始化，按钮当然不会出现。

[Bug]: ### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Messages sent through Azure Bot Test in Web Chat reach the OpenClaw Microsoft Teams webhook and receive replies, but messages sent through the installed Microsoft Teams app do not produce inbound gateway activity or replies.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install and log in with `@microsoft/teams.cli@preview`.
2. Create the Teams app using the Azure-backed bot flow:

`teams app create --name "Boardkeeper" --endpoint "https://openclaw.MyUrl.com/api/messages" --azure --subscription "<subscription-id>" --resource-group "rgname" --region "westeurope"`

3. Use the CLI output credentials to configure OpenClaw `channels.msteams` with the generated `CLIENT_ID`, `CLIENT_SECRET`, and `TENANT_ID`.
4. Start/restart OpenClaw 2026.4.24 with the Teams webhook configured at `/api/messages`.
5. Confirm `openclaw channels status --deep` shows `Microsoft Teams default: enabled, configured, running`.
6. In Azure Portal, open the Azure Bot resource created by the Teams CLI and use `Test in Web Chat`.
7. Observe that Web Chat messages reach OpenClaw and receive replies.
8. Install the same generated Teams app in Microsoft Teams.
9. Send a direct message to the installed Teams app.
10. Observe that no inbound Microsoft Teams activity appears in the OpenClaw gateway logs and no reply is posted in Teams.

### Expected behavior

Messages sent to the installed Microsoft Teams app should reach the same OpenClaw webhook endpoint and receive replies, as Azure Bot Test in Web Chat does with the same endpoint.

### Actual behavior

Azure Bot Test in Web Chat reaches the OpenClaw webhook and receives replies. Messages sent from the installed Microsoft Teams app do not produce visible inbound gateway activity and do not receive replies.

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.24

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04 on Azure VM

### Install method

npm global, running as a systemd user service

### Model

openai-codex/gpt-5.5

### Provider / routing chain

Microsoft Teams app -> Azure Bot / Bot Framework -> HTTPS endpoint -> Caddy reverse proxy -> OpenClaw msteams webhook -> OpenClaw gateway -> openai-codex/gpt-5

### Additional provider/model setup details

The public messaging endpoint is served over HTTPS by Caddy:

https://openclaw.MyURL.com/api/messages

Caddy proxies /api/messages* to the local OpenClaw Teams webhook on 127.0.0.1:3978.

The Azure network security group allows inbound HTTPS 443 from the AzureBotService service tag. Port 3978 is not exposed publicly.

Relevant redacted OpenClaw config:

channels.msteams.enabled: true
channels.msteams.appId: <azure-bot-app-id>
channels.msteams.appPassword: <redacted>
channels.msteams.tenantId: <tenant-id>
channels.msteams.webhook.port: 3978
channels.msteams.webhook.path: /api/messages
channels.msteams.dmPolicy: open
channels.msteams.groupPolicy: open
Routing binding during the test:

boardkeeper <- msteams

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Observed working path:

Azure Portal -> Azure Bot -> Test in Web Chat sends a message.
OpenClaw/Caddy receives the request on /api/messages.
The agent replies in Azure Web Chat.
Observed failing path:

The same bot/app is installed in Microsoft Teams.
A direct message is sent to the Teams app.
No inbound Microsoft Teams activity is visible in OpenClaw gateway logs.
No reply appears in Teams.
openclaw channels status --deep reports:

Gateway reachable.
Microsoft Teams default: enabled, configured, running.
Azure Bot diagnostics reported:

Azure bot discovered.
Bot registered.
Bot location: Azure.
Azure bot discoverable.
Teams channel enabled.
Endpoint: https://openclaw.MyUrl.com/api/messages.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected users/systems/channels:

Microsoft Teams users using the OpenClaw Microsoft Teams channel through an Azure Bot resource.

Severity:

High for Teams usage, because Teams chat messages do not reach the agent.

Frequency:

Observed on each tested Teams DM attempt in this setup.

Consequence:
